name: Old Cemetery
notes: Contains graves of 40 Revolutionary War soldiers. Massachusetts Historical Commission MACRIS MEN803.

name: East Norwalk Historical Cemetery
access_notes: Sometimes locked; managed by local taxing district
features: First Settlers of Norwalk Memorial (lists 31 individuals from 1649)
Grave of Thomas 'Yankee Doodle' Fitch IV (1724-1795)
notes: Oldest cemetery in Norwalk; triangle-shaped; near East Norwalk railway station. Mary Willet Hooker Buckingham buried here, near center of burying ground near East Norwalk railway station.

name: Ancient Burying Ground
notes: Adjacent to Center Church. Earliest surviving identifiable gravestone dates to 1648. Resting place of many Hartford founders including Rev. Thomas Hooker (exact location unknown).

name: Memento Mori Cemetery
notes: Rev. Samuel Hooker buried here; no stone survives.

name: Milford Cemetery
notes:
Active burial ground for Milford founding families. Mary Hooker Newton (H00003) buried here in what was historically called 'Peter Prudden's Garden' — no tombstone survives for her. John Newton (H00014), Lydia Ford Newton (I00012), Samuel Newton (H00011), and Ezekiel Newton (H00015) also buried here. Prudden was first minister of Milford.
GPS from FindAGrave (H00011 Samuel Newton burial).

name: Ancient Little Neck Cemetery (Bullock's Cove)
notes: Burial site of Capt. Thomas Willet; rough stone with rude inscription marks grave. Also known as Ancient Little Neck Cemetery / Rhode Island Historical Cemetery East Providence #5. Thomas Willet Sr. (b. 29 Aug 1605, d. 4 Aug 1674) buried here — has a famous memorial marker. Willet was the first Mayor of New York City.
alt_names: Rhode Island Historical Cemetery East Providence #5

name: Hillside Cemetery
notes: Old graveyard. Source: FindAGrave and Families of Ancient New Haven.

name: Christian Lane Cemetery
notes: Kensington is a parish of Berlin, CT — sources may list burial location as either 'Kensington' or 'Berlin'. John Hooker No. 2 (H00098) and Mercy Hart Hooker (I00041) buried here. Burial site of Samuel Hooker (H00086) and family

name: Grove Street Cemetery
notes: Historic New Haven cemetery. Hezekiah Stanley (d. Sept 11, 1860) and Sally Hooker Stanley (d. Dec 21, 1862) buried here.

name: Wethersfield Village Cemetery
notes: One of the oldest cemeteries in Connecticut. Contains gravestone of Sarah Standley Hooker (I00050), carved by William Stanclift, died June 15, 1726 aged 36. Pilot102: David Hills (TD0192) and his wife/cousin Amelia Hanmer Talcott Hills (T00794) buried here.

name: Fairview Cemetery
notes: Helen Hooker Talcott Stanley (TD0050) buried here. She was a major benefactor of the New Britain Museum of American Art — the museum her father John Butler Talcott (H03203) founded in 1903 is a short distance away. She donated the Helen T. and Phillip B. Stanley Gallery.

name: Village Green Cemetery
notes: Historic cemetery on the Guilford Village Green. Burial place of Hon. James Hooker (H00031).

name: Orange Center Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Elias Clark (HD0090), son of Sarah Newton Clark (H00138).

name: Center Church on the Green Churchyard
alt_names: First Church of Christ Burial Ground
notes: Historic underground crypt beneath Center Church on the New Haven Green, with associated outdoor burial ground. Burial place of Martha Newton Whittlesey (H00143) and Elizabeth Susanna Whittlesey Barnes (HD0101).

name: Titicus Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Rev. Jonathan Ingersoll (HD0115), Pastor of First Church Ridgefield 40 years, French & Indian War chaplain.

name: Episcopal Burying Ground
notes: Burial place of Mary Bryan (HD0188), daughter of Joseph Bryan Jr. (H00158). Inscription: "In memory of Mary, daughter of Mr. Joseph and Mehitabel Bryan, who died Nov. 1, A.D. 1753, aged ten years and ten days."

name: Rimmon Hill Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Susannah Stone Clark (HD0191), daughter of Ezekiel Stone (H00052) and Hannah Merwin.

name: Evergreen Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Horace B. Burwell (HD0212) and wife Mary Juliana Beach. Shared headstone.

name: O'ahu Cemetery
notes: One of Honolulu's oldest cemeteries. Contains graves of multiple generations of the Samuel Gelston Dwight (HD2376) family — Hooker descendants who settled in Hawaii during the missionary era. Also burial site for ABCFM missionaries, Hawaiian royalty, and prominent figures of the Kingdom of Hawaii era.

name: Metairie Cemetery
notes: One of the most elaborate garden cemeteries in the United States, built on the former Metairie Race Course. Notable for ornate above-ground tombs characteristic of New Orleans burial tradition.

name: Granary Burying Ground
notes: One of Boston's oldest cemeteries. Contains graves of Samuel Adams, John Hancock, Paul Revere, and victims of the Boston Massacre.

name: Cedar Hill Cemetery
notes: Major Hartford garden cemetery. Contains graves of J.P. Morgan Sr., Mark Twain, and many prominent Hartford families.

name: Princeton Cemetery
notes: Historic cemetery associated with Princeton University. Contains graves of Aaron Burr Sr., Aaron Burr Jr., Grover Cleveland, and many Princeton presidents.

name: Congressional Cemetery
notes: Historic cemetery established by Christ Church Washington Parish. Contains graves of numerous members of Congress, J. Edgar Hoover, and John Philip Sousa.

name: The Woodlands Cemetery
notes: Garden cemetery on the grounds of the historic Woodlands estate. One of Philadelphia's most distinguished rural cemeteries. Absorbed duplicate CEM847 Stage 6Y-690 per compiler ruling.

name: Trinity Church Cemetery and Mausoleum
notes: The only remaining active cemetery in Manhattan. Contains graves of John James Audubon, Alfred Tennyson Dickens, and members of the Morgan, Satterlee, and Astor families.

name: Austinburg Center Cemetery
notes: Cemetery of the Congregational church founded by Rev. Giles Hooker Cowles. Contains graves of Rev. Cowles, his wife Sallie White Cowles, and son Edward Giles Hooker Cowles.

name: Highwood Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Guy Vernon Wight (HD0823) and Lois Amine Krewson Wight (X01405).

name: Center Street Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Rev. Samuel Whittlesey (X01900), pastor of Wallingford 1710-1752.

name: West Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Rev. John Hart (I00137) and his first wife Rebekah Hubbard. FindAGrave Memorial #61366888.

name: Oakwood Cemetery
notes: Burial place of John Paine Cushman (X01916), Maria Jones Tallmadge Cushman (HD1033), Harriet Delafield Cushman Balch (HD2816), and Rev. John Paine Cushman Jr. (HD2817).

name: Washington Street Cemetery
notes: Burial place of John Delafield III (I00169) and Harriet Wadsworth Tallmadge Delafield (HD1035).

name: Saint Matthews Episcopal Church Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Sarah Taliaferro Cameron Labouisse (X00881) and John Witherspoon Labouisse (HD2605).

name: Serenity Gardens Memorial Park
notes: Burial place of Wyllys McDonald Ransom (HD2703) and Donna Elizabeth Lobaugh Ransom (X00930).

name: Green-Wood Cemetery
notes: Historic Brooklyn cemetery. Burial place of Dr. Charles Lancelot Minor (HD1607) and Mary McDowell Venable Minor (X00990).

name: Arlington National Cemetery
notes: National military cemetery. Burial place of Dr. John Minor (HD2760), WW1 veteran. Absorbed duplicate CEM475 Stage 6Y-691 per compiler ruling; the nation’s preeminent military cemetery — every attached ID verified retained in the merge audit.

name: Evergreen Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Rev. John Erskine Edwards (HD2631), Ann R. Hammond Edwards (X01927), and Erskine Mason Phelps (X01928).

name: Huntington Rural Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Chandler Dymick Ingersoll (HD1877).

name: Alder Brook Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Dr. Nathaniel Ruggles (X00282) and Rev. Thomas Ruggles Sr. (X01929).

name: Town Line Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Azubah Norton Bartlett (H00405) and Deacon Ichabod Bartlett Jr. (X00205).

name: Brick Church Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Hooker Bartlett (HD0436) and Hannah Baldwin Bartlett (X00206).

name: Weybridge Hill Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Judge Joseph Hayward (X01947), Amanda Murray Hayward (X01943), Marion Eliza Wilder Drake (X01952), PVT Henry Harrison Wilder (X01953).

name: Woodbury Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Orlando Wilder (X01948).

name: Pleasant Hill Cemetery
notes: Burial place of Franklin Hooker (X01955).

name: Mount Carmel Burying Ground
notes: Burial place of Job Lucianus Munson (H00455).

name: Saint Michael's Churchyard
notes: Burial place of E.L. Godkin (X01190). He died nearby at the home of a friend.

name: Dickinson Family Cemetery
notes: Burial site of Eli Foote (X02032). His name is also commemorated on a monument in the Foote-Ward Cemetery, Guilford CT (CEM066).

name: The Evergreens Cemetery
notes: Burial site of Edward Hooker (H01628), compiler of Descendants of Rev. Thomas Hooker (1909).

name: Woodland Cemetery
notes: Block 12, Lot 00247.

name: Riverside Cemetery
notes: Burial site of Sen. Francis Gillette (I00173) and family.

name: Jay Family Cemetery
notes: On the grounds of the Jay Estate, childhood home of John Jay, first Chief Justice of the United States. National Historic Landmark.

name: Green-Wood Cemetery
notes: Section 34, Lot 8379 — John Brooks Henderson (I00287) and wife Mary Foote Henderson (HD3149). Also Eunice Newton Foote (HD3148).

name: Palisado Cemetery
notes: Colonial-era cemetery on Palisado Avenue, Windsor CT. Burial place of Eunice Talcott Hooker (I00054), daughter of Governor Joseph Talcott, and James Hooker (H00348). Per Henry Reed Stiles, Stiles Family in America (1895), Job Stiles (H00462) and Mary Drake Stiles (I00624) were buried 'on one stone' here in what the 1895 book called the 'Old Windsor Graveyard' — the Palisado is the formal modern name.

name: Portland Burying Ground
notes: Established as Chatham burying ground; town renamed Portland 1841.

name: Fairview Cemetery
notes: Fairview Cemetery, West Hartford CT. Burial site of Alice Dudley Talcott Enders (TD0064), her husband Ostrom Enders (I00181), and the Talcott sisters Emeline Frances Talcott (H03213, 1849-1952, age 102) and Sarah Whiting Talcott (H03214, 1852-1936) — daughters of Henry Talcott (H01792) and Elizabeth Wells Whiting Talcott (X02416).

name: Mount Auburn Cemetery
notes: Historic garden cemetery, Cambridge MA. Plot: Azalea Garden, Lot 11002, Mausoleum 20.

name: Baptist Burial Ground
notes: Center Sandwich, Carroll County NH. Burial site of Ruth DeWitt Pratt Ives (I00188).

name: Woodlawn Cemetery
notes: Historic garden cemetery, Bronx NY. Burial site of T01463 James Frederick Talcott (Cypress Plot, Sec 47, Grave 1) and I00193 Louise Simmons Talcott.

name: Old Center Cemetery
notes: Burial site of T01389 Samuel Talcott III, H00687 Abigail Pantry Hooker, H01790 Mary Talcott, H01792 Henry Talcott. West Hartford CT.

name: North Cemetery
notes: Burial site of H01788 Samuel Talcott IV. West Hartford CT.

name: Bridgehampton Cemetery
notes: Bridgehampton, Suffolk County NY. Burial site of H01787 Eliza Talcott Francis, X01822 Rev. Amzi Francis, and their son Roger A. Francis. The Presbyterian Church at Bridgehampton was Rev. Francis's parish from 1823.

name: Elmwood Cemetery
notes: Vernon (Rockville), Tolland County CT. Burial site of T01031 Deacon Phineas Talcott and wife Hannah Kellogg (I00195), T01063 Ralph Talcott and wife Susan Bull (I00196).

name: Grove Hill Cemetery
notes: Rockville, Tolland County CT. Burial site of T01072 George Talcott and his wife I00198 Rosanna McLean Talcott.

name: Old Burial Ground of North Bolton (Bamforth Road)
notes: Bamforth Road, Bolton CT (also known as North Bolton Cemetery). Burial site of T00909 Deacon Benjamin Talcott (1725-1811) and I00207 Elizabeth Lyman Talcott (1730-1810). Gravestone inscription for Elizabeth: "Sacred to the memory of Mrs. Elisabeth; consort of Dea. Benjamin Talcott; who departed this life Sept 3rd 1810, in the 81st year of her age. She opened her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue was the law of kindness. Her children [arise] up, and call her blessed."

name: Quarryville Cemetery
notes: Bolton, Tolland County CT. Burial site of T01029 Deacon Elijah Talcott, U00049 Rebecca Olmstead, and Wealthy Strong.

name: Mount Hope Cemetery
notes: Talcottville (Vernon), Tolland County CT. Burial site of T01043 Deacon Flavel Talcott and family.

name: Ellington Center Cemetery
notes: Ellington, Tolland County CT. Burial site of Horace W. Kibbe (I00205).

name: Winsted Old Burying Ground
notes: Winsted, Litchfield County CT. Burial site of T01047 Dr. William Olmstead Talcott.

name: Wooster Cemetery
notes: Danbury, Fairfield County CT. Burial site of I00206 Elizabeth Wilcox (wife of T01047) and T01081 Elizabeth O. Talcott.

name: Southwest Cemetery
notes: Vernon (North Bolton), Tolland County CT. Burial site of T01052 Samuel Smith Talcott and his wife Harriet Smith Talcott. Also T01030 Deacon Samuel Talcott and wife Sarah Smith.

name: Cider Brook Cemetery
notes: Avon, Hartford County CT. Burial site of I00212 Thomas Fletcher Oakes.

name: Lake View Cemetery
notes: Seattle, King County WA. Burial site of X02139 Walter Oakes and Mary Beekman Taylor Oakes. Inscription: "WALTER OAKES 1864-1911 and his wife MARY BEEKMAN TAYLOR 1866-1948."

name: Saint Andrew's Episcopal Church Cemetery
notes: Old St. Andrew's Church, 59 Tariffville Road, Bloomfield CT. Burial site of TD0053 Frances Stanley Stout and I00214 John Deming Stout Jr.

name: Hewitt Cemetery
notes: North Stonington, New London County CT. Hewitt family cemetery — Nina Brower Hewitt (I00215) was from the Hewitt family of this area. Burial site of TD0074 Talcott Stanley and I00215 Nina Brower Hewitt Stanley.

name: Cypress Cemetery
notes: Old Saybrook, Middlesex County CT. Burial site of X01001 Rev. Thomas Buckingham (1646-1709).

name: Log Plain Cemetery
notes: Greenfield, Franklin County MA. Burial site of T01032 Elizabeth Talcott Griswold and I00217 Theophilus Griswold.

name: Center Cemetery
notes: Bernardston, Franklin County MA. Burial site of T02202 Rev. John Flavel Griswold. John Flavel spent summer months in Bernardston in later life.

name: Old Willington Hill Cemetery
notes: Willington Hill, Tolland County CT. Burial site of T01033 Daniel Talcott (1762-1807).

name: Wethersfield Cemetery
notes: Kewanee (formerly Wethersfield settlement), Henry County IL. Named by Connecticut settlers who moved west in the 1830s-1840s — a direct echo of Wethersfield CT, the founding town of the Hooker/Talcott line. Burial site of T02215 Abigail Talcott Hubbard, I00224 Jeduthan Hubbard, and I00222 Abigail Holt Talcott (Daniel's widow who followed her daughter west).

name: Grace Cemetery
notes: Castile, Wyoming County NY. Burial site of T02229 Anson Howard, I00229 Hannah Hurd Howard, and T02230 Dr. Riley D. Howard.

name: Prospect Hill Cemetery
notes: Perry Center, Wyoming County NY. Burial site of T02227 Julia Howard Ward, I00233 Phichol Munroe Ward, T02236 Sidney Ward, and I00237 Emeline Morgan Ward.

name: Chilson Community Cemetery
notes: Chilson hamlet, Ticonderoga area, Essex County NY. Burial site of T02247 Daniel Smith and I00243 Clarissa Adaline Pearl Smith.

name: West Side Cemetery
notes: Guilford, New Haven County CT. Burial site of T02264 Dr. Alvan Talcott Jr. (1804-1891) and I00247 Olive Chittenden Talcott.

name: Spring Grove Cemetery
notes: Hartford, Hartford County CT. Burial site of T01458 Mary Corning Talcott Guyton (1859-1888) and X01824 Maria J. Corning Talcott (1832-1862). NOTE: distinct from Spring Grove Cemetery Cincinnati OH (CEM083).

name: East Cemetery
notes: Hartford County. Burial site of the Landfear family among others.

name: Rushford Cemetery
notes: Founded 1850. Elihu Talcott (T01057) was the first President of the Rushford Cemetery Association.

name: Oak Hill Cemetery
notes: Historic Georgetown cemetery. Burial site of multiple Talcott family members.

name: Hollywood Cemetery
notes: Historic Richmond cemetery, burial site of Confederate dead and notable Virginians. Charles Gratiot Talcott Sr. (T01839) buried here.

name: Pine Crest Cemetery
notes: Burial site of Hugh Gaylord Barclay Jr.

name: Forest Hills Memorial Park and Mausoleum
notes: Burial site of LT Edmund T. Barclay (Talcott descendant) and his wife Ruth.

name: National Memorial Park
notes: Burial site of Lawrence Barnett Huse (d. 1972, age 18).

name: Saint Anne's Cemetery
notes: Burial site of Rev. William Scott Southgate DD and his wife Harriet Randolph Talcott Southgate.

name: Ware Episcopal Church Cemetery
notes: Ware Episcopal Church Cemetery — burial site of multiple Hollerith descendants and their spouses.

name: Leeds Episcopal Church Cemetery
notes: Leeds Episcopal Church Cemetery — burial site of Herman Hollerith III and his wife Agnes Newton Hollerith.

name: Albany Rural Cemetery
notes: Major rural cemetery north of Albany NY; final resting place of President Chester A. Arthur and many notable New Yorkers. Talcott burials include T01818 Bvt. Lt. Col. George Henry Talcott (USMA 1831, Mexican War, wounded Molino del Rey), I00282 Catharine Jane Starke Talcott (his wife), T01819 Brig. Gen. Sebastian Visscher Talcott (compiler of the Talcott Genealogy book), and I00281 Olivia Maria Shearman Talcott (his wife).

name: Boxwood Cemetery
notes: Long-standing Medina NY cemetery serving Orleans County. The Hebner-Cook family plot complex includes David Conrad Hebner (1863-1930), his wife Lucy M. Hooker Hebner (1864-1924), their daughter Etta E. Hebner Cook (1896-1957), her husband Irving Burns Cook (1895-1968), and their two sons Clarence Irving 'Cookie' Cook (1918-1977) and Donald David 'Cookie' Cook (1922-2004), plus Donald's wife Shirley Marie Perry Cook (1936-1967). In 2016, a CENOTAPH for PVT Jay David Hebner (1895-1918, KIA WWI in France, actually buried at Saint-Mihiel American Cemetery) was added to the Hebner-Cook plot complex through the efforts of Jay's great-niece Debbie Morse (Dabrina D. Cook Morse) — Donald's daughter — restoring his memory to Medina after nearly 100 years of absence from the village.

name: Saint-Mihiel American Cemetery and Memorial
notes: World War I American cemetery in northeast France, just outside Thiaucourt-Regniéville (a commune in the Meurthe-et-Moselle department). Approximately 4,153 American soldiers are buried here — most killed in the Saint-Mihiel offensive (September 12-15, 1918) and subsequent operations. Operated by the American Battle Monuments Commission. The Hooker-line connection: PVT Jay David Hebner (HD3254, great-grandson of Thomas Edwin Hooker) is interred here at Plot B, Row 24, Grave 31. He was killed September 17, 1918 — two days after the main offensive ended — while serving with the 309th Infantry Regiment, 78th Division, U.S. Army.

name: Stafford Rural Cemetery
notes: Rural cemetery in Stafford, Genesee County NY (near Le Roy). Burial place of the Thomas Harvey Hooker branch — his family migrated from Murray Orleans County to Stafford Genesee County late in life. Thomas Harvey Hooker (H03872, 1840-1919), his wife Caroline E. 'Carrie' Shader Hooker (X02269, 1842-1923), their daughter Alva J. Hooker (H05031, 1869-1953), her husband Jean Mason Hooker (X02274, 1869-1914), and their infant son H. Mason Hooker (HD3258, 1906-1906) are all buried here in adjacent plots. The cemetery thus contains four generations of one Hooker line — gen 8 (Thomas Harvey + Caroline) → gen 9 (Alva) → gen 10 (H. Mason).

name: Mount Hope Cemetery
notes: One of America's oldest municipal Victorian cemeteries (founded 1838 — pre-dating the famous Mt. Auburn). The Gordon family plot is in Lot 61 Range 2 — purchased by Luther Gordon Sr. (Brockport bank founder, 1818-1881). His son George Cooley Gordon Sr. (X02281, NOTABLE) paid $9,229.54 for perpetual care of the plot in 1883. The plot features a stunning monument with columns and an allegorical female figure with drapery, one hand across her heart and the other holding a cross, gazing into the distance. Modest stones below in a square perimeter — except for infant William 'Willie' Gordon's stone (HD3268, 1879-1880) prominently set below the central angel monument. Hooker-line burials at Mt. Hope: George Cooley Gordon Sr (X02281), Ida May Hooker Gordon (H03892, with monument), William 'Willie' Gordon (HD3268), George Cooley Gordon II (HD3262), Cecelia Mitchell Kimball Gordon (X02282), George Cooley Gordon III (HD3263), Cornelia Macy Hawks Gordon (X02283), Cecilia 'Trottie' Gordon (HD3264), Laura Kimball Gordon (HD3265), Fred Hooker Gordon Sr (HD3266) with separate Section MM Lot 266 plot, Florence Robinson Gordon (X02284), Fred Hooker Gordon Jr (HD-prefix), Virginia DeVilbiss Gordon (X-prefix), Fred Hooker Gordon III (HD-prefix, infant Boston meningioma), Lucius Robinson 'Bob' Gordon (HD-prefix, NOTABLE), Marie Antoinette 'Tony' Townson Gordon (X-prefix), William Ward Gordon (HD-prefix), Emilie Wolcott Gordon (X-prefix), William Ward Gordon Jr (HD-prefix back in original Lot 61 Range 2). Largest Hooker-tree concentration after Cypress Lawn SF.

name: Lakeview Cemetery
notes: Brockport Monroe County NY cemetery. Concentrated burials from the Brockway/Robinson side of the Hooker tree — specifically the Helen Shader Brockway and Louise Estella Robinson Carter branches. Hooker-line burials: Helen M. Shader Brockway (HD3274), Walter Oshrin Brockway (X-prefix above, her husband), Etta May Brockway Boehm (HD-prefix above, their daughter), Louise Estella Robinson Carter (HD-prefix above), Charles Joseph Carter (X-prefix above, English immigrant). All five cluster here while their cousins/siblings buried elsewhere (Beach Ridge, Saint Mary's Medina, Mount Hope Rochester, Mount Albion Albion).

name: Mount Albion Cemetery
notes: Mount Albion Cemetery, Albion Orleans Co NY. Burial site of Emma Louisa Hooker Brockway (H03874, 1846-1940 age 93). Different cemetery from her cousin Helen Shader Brockway who is buried at Lakeview Brockport.

name: Woodlawn Memorial Park
notes: Colma CA cemetery. Receiving destination for many remains transferred from defunct San Francisco cemeteries during the 1900s SF cemetery removals (when SF banned new burials and most existing burials were moved south to Colma). Mass-grave destinations for many transferred remains. Hooker-tree connection: William Mandeville Lent (X-prefix above, NOTABLE+EASTER_EGG, California 49er) — originally buried Masonic Cemetery SF; remains presumably moved here in mass grave per Findagrave; specific disposition of ashes 'unknown.'

name: Holy Cross Catholic Cemetery
notes: Holy Cross Catholic Cemetery, Colma CA. Largest Catholic cemetery in San Francisco Bay Area. Hooker tree connection: the Eugene Lent family (Catholic side of the Lent family). Hooker-line burials: Berthe Marion Welch Lent (X02302, Eugene's wife). Daughters Frances Welch Lent Porter (1901-1947) and Ruth D'Alte Lent Hart (1903-1981) also buried here but intentionally NOT BUILT in tree per Hooker-centric scope. Eugene himself was buried at Cypress Lawn (Protestant) rather than Holy Cross (Catholic) — possibly indicating he was not Catholic and wife converted or married out of faith.

name: Forest Home Cemetery
notes: Forest Home Cemetery, Milwaukee WI — oldest continuously-operating cemetery in Milwaukee. Major burial cluster of the David Griswold Hooker (H02448) Mayor of Milwaukee family across 3 generations: David himself + 2nd wife Julia Almira Ashley Hooker (X02310); David's eldest son Edward Harris Hooker (H03908, lawyer, Section 33) + his wife Grace Lawton Hooker + their son Ralph D. Hooker (1895-1922 d.young); David's daughter Alice Ashley McCutcheon (H03911) + husband Frank C. McCutcheon; David's daughters Julia E. Hooker (H03912) + Edith Mary Greenwood (H03913). The Hooker presence at Forest Home spans 1888 (David's mayoral years) through 1975 (Edith's death) — nearly a century.

name: Hillside Cemetery
notes: Hillside Cemetery, Castleton Rutland Co VT. Major Truman Hooker family burial site. Hooker tree: Sarah Jane Hooker Ainsworth (H02447, gen 7) + husband John Elijah Ainsworth + their 6 children (H03902-H03907). Most of the Ainsworth family clustered here.

name: Middlebury Cemetery
notes: Middlebury Cemetery, Middlebury Addison Co VT. Edward Payson Hooker family + David G. Hooker's first wife. Hooker tree: Sarah Parker Harris Hooker (X-prefix, David G's first wife, d.1868 age 30); Edward Clarendon Hooker (H03917, m. Ruth Fairchild); Emily Griswold Hooker (H03916).

name: Melrose Abbey Memorial Park
notes: Melrose Abbey Memorial Park, Anaheim Orange Co CA. Edward Payson Hooker family West Coast burials: Bessie Robbins Hooker (H03914, 1872-1960), David Ashley Hooker (H03919, 1879-1951) + wife Margery Cordelia Cooper Hooker (1891-1963). Two Edward Payson children migrated to Orange Co CA in later life.

name: Pine Grove Cemetery
notes: Pine Grove Cemetery, Rathdrum Kootenai Co Idaho. Stuart Van Rensselaer Hooker (H03915, surgeon, 1874-1948) + wife Elida Wright Hooker (1886-1930). Old Section Block 10 Lot 7. Idaho is unusual destination — Stuart was based Seattle WA but chose burial in Idaho with his Spokane-born wife.

name: Airmont Coptic Cemetery
notes: Airmont Coptic Cemetery, Airmont Rockland Co NY. Charles Grandison Fairchild II (X-prefix above, 1843-1933 NOTABLE+EASTER_EGG, 2nd Rollins College President + Civil War vet).

name: Ferncliff Cemetery and Mausoleum
notes: Ferncliff Cemetery and Mausoleum, Hartsdale Westchester Co NY. Ruth Fairchild Hooker (X-prefix above, 1882-1957). Major NY metro cemetery with celebrity burials (Joan Crawford, Judy Garland, Ed Sullivan, etc.)

name: Rockefeller Family Cemetery
address: Pocantico Hills section of the Rockefeller estate, Sleepy Hollow NY
notes: Private family cemetery on the Pocantico Hills section of the Rockefeller estate (the larger 3,400-acre family estate near Tarrytown / Sleepy Hollow / Mount Pleasant Westchester Co NY). Burials of the Rockefeller family across multiple generations including John D. Rockefeller Sr. (founder Standard Oil), JDR Jr. (X-prefix), Abby Aldrich Rockefeller (X-prefix), JDR III (X-prefix), and Blanchette Ferry Hooker Rockefeller (HD3284 — the Hooker tree's only Sleepy Hollow burial via her marriage). Generally not open to the public. The cemetery sits on what was the Pocantico Hills estate, where multiple Rockefeller-family residences clustered including Kykuit + Fieldwood Farm (where Blanchette + JDR III lived in their later years).

name: Sawyer Hill Burying Ground
notes: Historic burying ground in Newburyport MA, Marquand family burial site. Ground used by Newburyport Hooker descendants and the Marquand family lineage. Both John Phillips Marquand (X02321, d.1960) + his wife Adelaide Ferry Hooker Marquand (H04640, d.1963) buried here.

name: Dennis Village Cemetery
notes: Cape Cod cemetery in Dennis MA. Burial site of Elon Huntington Hooker 'Lonny' Marquand (HD3307, d.2021), grandson of Elon Huntington Hooker (H03434), and connected to Cape Cod Hooker family residences (Lonny's wife Jean MacMurtry attended Dennis Union Church for their wedding; the couple later moved to East Orleans, also on Cape Cod).

name: Lakewood Cemetery
notes: Historic Minneapolis cemetery established 1871. Burial site of Bruce Bliss Dayton (X02340, 1918-2015), founder of B. Dalton Booksellers + CEO/Chairman of Dayton-Hudson Corporation (later Target Corporation). Many members of the Dayton retail family are interred here.

name: Oak Hill Cemetery
notes: Historic Georgetown cemetery established 1849; one of Washington DC's most prominent burial grounds, designed in the rural-cemetery tradition. Burial site of Sen. Charles Harting Percy (X02342, 1919-2011), 4-term U.S. Senator from Illinois + Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee. Notable Percy's are also buried here.

name: LittleVille Cemetery
notes: Avon NY cemetery containing the Pierrepont-Ward family burials of the Rochester branch (Jonathan Edwards Pierrepont HD1092, his daughter Caroline Editha Pierpont Ward HD1545, and her husband Rev. George Kemp Ward X01665).

name: Lakeview Cemetery
notes: Groveland NY cemetery. Burial site of Charles Sloan Ward (HD3402), his second wife Rose Marguerita Boothe (I00341), and their daughter Mildred May Ward (HD3406). Distinct from CEM154 Lakeview Cemetery in Brockport NY.

name: Riverside Cemetery
notes: Rochester NY cemetery. Burial site of Nellie May Wyness Ward Hoppough (I00340), Charles Sloan Ward's first wife.

name: Old Eastbury Cemetery
notes: Glastonbury CT cemetery. Burial site of Col. Samuel Talcott (T01001, 1732-1780), whose Revolutionary War-era gravestone survives intact. His first wife Mary Smith Talcott (U00042, d.1770) is unconfirmed but per family tradition likely buried here too, having predeceased him by ten years. Resting place of John House IV (X01233, 1758-1798, age 39-40) — husband of Esther Hooker House. He died in Hartford in November 1798 leaving Esther a widow at thirty-nine with seven living children (and Cyprian an infant of about eighteen months). She lived another fifty years and died in 1848 at age eighty-nine. Esther herself is buried at Old Eastbury per FG memorial — though her primary residence shifted to East Hartford after John IV's death. [Merged from duplicate CEM246, Stage 6Y-652.]

name: Cheney Cemetery
notes: Manchester Connecticut cemetery associated with the Cheney silk-manufacturing family. Burial site of Walter Hooker Olcott (H03350, 1867-1936) and his wife Adeline Louise Cheney Olcott (I00355, 1870-1955) — the marriage that brought the Olcott agricultural-line and Cheney silk-manufacturing-line families together in Manchester.

name: East Cemetery
notes: Manchester Connecticut municipal cemetery. Burial site of Polly Olcott Belden (HD3416) and her husband Donald L. Belden (I00356); Roger Olcott (HD3417) and both of his wives Janet Jessup Olcott (I00357) and Dorothy Denton Olcott (I00358).

name: Berlin Corner Cemetery
notes: Berlin Corner Cemetery, Berlin Corners Vermont. Burial site of the Perrin-Hobart founding family of Berlin VT — Zachariah Perrin Esq. (X01832, Berlin VT 1789 founder, Rev War vet, d.1838 age 90), his wife Mary Talcott Perrin (T01387, d.1828), their daughter Betsey Perrin Hobart (TD0120, d.1876), her husband Rev. James Hobart (X02368, Berlin VT first settled minister, d.1862 age 95), their son David Hobart (TD0129, d.1901), and his wife Caroline Reed Hobart (X02370, d.1898). Three Perrin-Hobart generations. [pilot100] Backfilled with Evans/Yale Berlin VT generation: Dr. Ira Evans Sr. (X02383) and Emeline Hobart Evans (TD0124, daughter of Rev. James + Betsey Hobart); their seven children including 4 died young/childhood/infancy (Mary E., Ira Jr., J. Annette, William Dwight) and 3 adult-lifespan (Mary Emeline Evans Yale + her Civil War vet husband Capt. Thomas Blossom Yale 108th NY Inf, Edward P. Evans 10th VT Inf wounded Cold Harbor, and Civil War Medal of Honor recipient Ira Hobart Evans TD0163 who died San Diego CA 1922 and was returned to Berlin Corner per his wishes). Cemetery now spans four generations of Hobart-Evans-Yale family across ~150 years.

name: Worcester Village Cemetery
notes: Worcester Village Cemetery, Worcester Vermont. Burial site of James Hobart Jr. (TD0122, 1807-1897) who wrote the c.1881 biography of his father Rev. James Hobart preserved by Vermont Genealogy. His wife Sophia Strickland Hobart (1807-1902) and several children also buried here — not yet built into JSON.

name: Westminster New Cemetery
notes: Westminster New Cemetery, Westminster Vermont. Burial site of Julia Hobart Barnard (TD0128, 1819-1908) and her husband Rev. Pliny Fisk Barnard (X02369, 1820-1908). Their daughter Harriet Newell Barnard Pierce (1860-1952) and her husband Sibley A. Pierce (1851-1925) also buried here — not yet built into JSON.

name: Fairview Cemetery
notes: Fairview Cemetery, Pampa Texas — burial site of Pampa founder Timothy Dwight Hobart (TD0133, 1855-1935) and his wife Minnie Wood Warren Hobart (X02371, 1859-1949). Vermont-to-Texas migration grave. Their son Warren Dwight Hobart (TD0134, 1891-1910 died_young) also buried here.

name: Green Mount Cemetery
notes: Green Mount Cemetery, Montpelier Vermont — primary burial ground of the Vermont Dewey dynasty. Burial site of Dr. Julius Yemans Dewey (X02373, NLI founder) and his first wife Mary Polly Perrin Dewey (TD0138); their eldest son Charles Dewey (TD0139) and his wife Betsey Tarbox (X02374) and their seven children TD0143-TD0149; second son Edward Dewey (TD0140) and his wife Susan Griggs Lilley (X02375). Three generations of Deweys in a single cemetery. GPS Latitude 44.257843, Longitude -72.5944519.

name: Washington National Cathedral (Bethlehem Chapel)
notes: Washington National Cathedral (Cathedral Church of Saint Peter and Saint Paul), Washington DC. Bethlehem Chapel crypt level holds the remains of Adm. George Dewey (TD0141, transferred 1925 from Arlington by his widow) and Mildred McLean Hazen Dewey (X02380, buried alongside him 1931).

name: Proprietors Burying Ground (South Cemetery)
notes: Proprietors Burying Ground (also called South Cemetery), Portsmouth New Hampshire. Burial of Susan Boardman Goodwin Dewey (X02379, Adm. Dewey's first wife, d.1872 age 28 of childbirth complications) and her son George Goodwin Dewey (TD0157, Adm. Dewey's only child, d.1963 age 90 — cremated Chicago, ashes returned to family lot here). Goodwin family lot.

name: Morningside Cemetery
notes: Morningside Cemetery, Brattleboro Vermont. Burial site of US District Judge James Loren Martin (X02377, Wikipedia notable Vermont Speaker), his second wife Jessie Dewey Martin (TD0151), and their daughter Margaret Susan Martin Tucker (TD0156, Vermont Republican National Committee member 1932-1936). Three generations of Vermont Republican political family.

name: South Church Cemetery (South Cemetery)
notes: South Church Cemetery (also called South Cemetery), Portsmouth New Hampshire. Burial site of Gov. Ichabod Goodwin (X02381, 27th Governor of New Hampshire 1859-1861, Wikipedia notable, easter-egg orbit per pilot99 compiler override). His wife Sarah Parker Rice Goodwin (X02382, d.1896 age 91) presumed buried alongside per FindAGrave. NOTE: Distinct from the Proprietors Burying Ground (CEM183) where their daughter Susan Boardman Goodwin Dewey is buried — Portsmouth NH has multiple historic South-named cemeteries.

name: Alton Cemetery
notes: Alton Cemetery, Alton (Madison County) Illinois. Burial of Francese Abi 'Fannie' Hurlbut Evans (X02385, 1838-1924, divorced first wife of Ira Hobart Evans) at her natal community, and her son Hobart Yale Evans (TD0166, 1876-1951, single, Austin TX real estate agent) who was removed from Texas to Illinois for burial alongside her in November 1951.

name: Oakwood Cemetery
notes: Oakwood Cemetery, Austin (Travis County) Texas. Burial of Francis Hurlbut Evans (TD0167, 1880-1963, WWI veteran, youngest son of Ira Hobart Evans) and his wife May Bess Orr Evans (X02388, 1883-1962). Three Ira Hobart Evans descendants buried in three different cemeteries spanning Texas, Illinois, and Vermont.

name: Williams Cemetery
notes: Williams Cemetery, Warren (Bradley County) Arkansas. Burial of Louise Matilda 'Lula' Perrin Frizzell (TD0174, 1862-1925, schoolteacher, daughter of William Alexander Perrin and Clara Niven Perrin) and her husband Dr. Daniel Harrison 'Douglas' Frizzell Jr. (X02391, 1858-1935, Vanderbilt-trained physician). Their five children's burials scattered across Arkansas, California, Oklahoma, and Tennessee.

name: Odd Fellows Rest Cemetery
notes: Odd Fellows Rest Cemetery, Aberdeen (Monroe County) Mississippi. Perrin family plot with Pronecy B. Tindall Perrin (X02389, 1808-1884, estranged wife of Berlin VT Presbyterian minister Rev. Truman Perrin) and her daughter Mary Caroline Perrin Pollard (TD0171, 1837-1866, predeceased mother by 18 years). Pronecy's 1884 burial was a continent away from her husband's 1869 East Road Cemetery Berlin VT burial — a marker of their long estrangement.

name: Mount Rest Cemetery
notes: Mount Rest Cemetery, Saint Johns, Clinton County, Michigan. Hooker-tree burials: MAJ Porter Kendrick Perrin (TD0184, 1833-1903), his wife Ella M. Sessions Perrin (X02397, 1846-1926, X-prefix spouse), their son Archer M. Perrin (TD0185, 1863-1927), and infant son Paul Rex Perrin (TD0186, 1869-1869). The Perrin Vermont-to-Michigan migration concludes here.

name: East Road Cemetery
notes: East Road Cemetery, Berlin, Washington County, Vermont. Hooker-tree burials: Rev. William Perrin (T01396, 1792-1824, son of Zachariah Perrin Esq. and Mary Talcott Perrin) and Rev. Truman Perrin (TD0170, 1796-1869, his younger brother). Both Congregational ministers; both born and died in Berlin VT.

name: Center Cemetery
notes: Center Cemetery, South Windsor, Hartford County, Connecticut. Hooker-tree burials: Epaphras Hills Jr. (TD0187, 1779-1869, son of Hannah Talcott Hills) and his wife Permelia Meacham Hills (X02400, 1779-1851).

name: Hockanum Cemetery
notes: Hockanum Cemetery, East Hartford, Hartford County, Connecticut. Hooker-tree burials: Epaphras Hills Sr. (X01833, 1759-1844, husband of Hannah Talcott Hills); Hannah Hills (TD0188, 1784-1878, age 93, their daughter); Talcott Hills (TD0193, 1803-1869, their son); and Talcott's wife Laura Brewer Hills (X02401, 1808-1854). The matriarch Hannah Talcott Hills (T01388, 1759-1848) died in East Hartford and is presumed buried here as well, though source confirmation is pending.

name: Plainmont Cemetery
notes: Plainmont Cemetery, East Montpelier, Washington County, Vermont. Hooker-tree burials: Rev. Justus Newton Perrin Jr. (TD0206, 1863-1940) and his first wife Laura Athelia Gale Perrin (X02411, 1864-1896, died in childbirth at age 32).

name: Lone Fir Pioneer Cemetery
notes: Lone Fir Pioneer Cemetery, Portland, Multnomah County, Oregon. Hooker-tree burials: George Edward Cole (TD0211, 6th Governor of Washington Territory, 1826-1906); his first wife Mary E. Cardwell Cole (X02418, 1836-1875); and their son George Frederick 'Fred' Cole (TD0213, 1858-1879, died age 21). George's second wife Annie Augusta King Cole (X02419, 1836-1913) chose to be buried at Congressional Cemetery in Washington DC instead, possibly reflecting George's congressional service.

name: Graham Cemetery
notes: Graham Cemetery, Hubbardsville, Madison County, New York. Hooker-tree burials: Sylvester Risley (TD0216, 1846-1920); his son-in-law James Sims Wratten (X02424, 1865-1938, born Cranbrook England); and Wratten's grandson-in-law Richard P. Stapleton (X02425, 1902-1976, husband of Alice Marie Wratten Stapleton). Three generations of the Risley-Wratten-Stapleton chain rest here — the New York anchor of the Hooker-Talcott-Hills line as it descends into the modern era.

name: Brethren Cemetery
notes: Brethren Cemetery in Panora, Guthrie County, Iowa. Burial place of Samuel Talcott Whitman (HD3420, 1855-1935) — the West Hartford Connecticut grandson of Sam IV who migrated to Iowa as a young man — and his Iowa-born wife Barbara Ellen Rodgers Whitman (X02437, 1859-1900). The Iowa anchor of the Talcott-Whitman line.

name: Talcott Cemetery
notes: Talcott Cemetery, Lanesborough, Berkshire County MA. The named family burying ground of the Capt. Nehemiah Talcott line in Lanesborough. Capt. Nehemiah migrated from Glastonbury CT around 1790 and the cemetery preserves three generations of his family. GPS 42.53106, -73.23031.

name: Riverside Cemetery
notes: Riverside Cemetery, Lanesborough, Berkshire County MA. Burial place of the Sherman branch of the Capt. Nehemiah Talcott line: Tirzah Ophelia Talcott Sherman, her husband Joel Bradley Sherman, their son George Bradley Sherman, and his New York-born wife Mary Jane Williams Sherman.

name: Aultorest Memorial Park
notes: Aultorest Memorial Park, Ogden, Weber County, Utah. Burial place of Allen Talcott Wood (T01490) — the Massachusetts grandson of Capt. Nehemiah Talcott who migrated west — and his Utah-native wife Charlotte Elizabeth Chase Wood (X02449). Their son Chuck Wood would migrate further to California.

name: Skyview Memorial Lawn
notes: Skyview Memorial Lawn, Vallejo, Solano County, California. Burial place of Charles Lawrence 'Chuck' Wood (T01492, b. Ogden UT 1898, d. Vallejo CA 1980) and his Utah-born wife Harriet Maud 'Peggy' Wilson Wood (X02450). Six generations from Capt. Nehemiah's Glastonbury Connecticut birthplace, the Talcott line ends here on the West Coast.

name: Woodmere Cemetery
notes: Woodmere Cemetery, Detroit, Wayne County, Michigan. Burial place of Edward A. Bromley (X02467, 1823-1891) — the Connecticut-born husband of Hooker descendant Elizabeth Benedict Bromley who migrated west to Detroit, where he died.

name: Hindes Cemetery
notes: Hindes Cemetery, Addison, Addison County VT. Burial place of Bela Norton (H01050) and his first wife Lydia Bushnell Norton (X02572). The couple migrated from Connecticut to Cheshire MA (1794) to Addison VT (where Lydia died 1813); Bela later moved on to Mexico NY where he died 1845, but his remains appear to have been returned to Addison.

name: Topeka Cemetery
notes: Topeka Cemetery, Topeka, Shawnee County, Kansas. Burial place of Henry E. Norton (HD3490, 1831-1892, the Kansas migrant), his wife Helen Rebecca Wilson Norton (X02593, 1844-1893), and their daughter Lucy Norton (HD3491, 1873-1896, who died of consumption in Wolcott CO).

name: Ely Cemetery
notes: Ely Cemetery, Lyme, New London County CT. Burial place of Rebecca Allis Anderson (H01061, 1780-1870), her son Samuel Pierce Anderson (HD3494, 1816-1898), and his wife Hannah Perkins Anderson (X02597, 1819-1894). The Anderson branch settled in Lyme on the Connecticut shore, several towns east of the Allis ancestral seat at East Guilford/Madison.

name: Evergreen Cemetery
notes: Evergreen Cemetery, Killingworth, Middlesex County CT. Burial place of Clark Wilson Coe (H02228, 1848-1919) and his wife Harriet Lamira Hull Coe (X02643, 1850-1915). Clark moved here from his Madison ancestral home, establishing a Coe presence in Killingworth that did not persist into the next generation — his daughter Minnie returned to Guilford and is buried at Alder Brook Cemetery (CEM047). Distinct from the multiple other Evergreen Cemeteries in CT (in New Haven, Stonington, Watertown, etc.).

name: Churches Corners Cemetery
notes: Churches Corners Cemetery, Wheatland Township, Hillsdale County, Michigan. Burial place of Anson Fowler (X02653, 1809-1855) and his wife Harriet Louisa Gridley Fowler (H02242, 1810-1898). The first major Hooker-line burial site in Michigan. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Oak Grove Cemetery
notes: Oak Grove Cemetery, St. Louis, Gratiot County, Michigan. Burial place of Edward Kirtland Fowler (H03720, 1832-1898) and his wife Eunice Tower Eddy Fowler (X02661, 1841-1928). Distinct from Oak Grove cemeteries elsewhere. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Sunset View Cemetery
notes: Sunset View Cemetery, Jonesville, Hillsdale County, Michigan. Burial place of Mary Louisa Fowler Dearing (H03721, 1834-1909) and her husband Cornelius Cuyler Dearing (X02662, 1819-1893). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Mount Hope Cemetery
notes: Mount Hope Cemetery, Lansing, Ingham County, Michigan. Burial place of Ann Amelia Fowler Fuller (H03723, 1841-1900) and her husband Henry Brigham Fuller (X02664, 1840-1902). Distinct from the Mount Hope Cemeteries in Talcottville CT (CEM120) and Rochester NY (CEM153). A cenotaph for Ann Amelia is also placed in her parents' Hillsdale plot at Churches Corners. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: River Ridge Cemetery
notes: River Ridge Cemetery, Belding Ionia County MI. Olger family burial site. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Coe (Pleasant Ridge) Cemetery
notes: Coe (Pleasant Ridge) Cemetery, Coe Township Isabella County MI. Multi-generational Fowler family burial site. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Salt River Cemetery
notes: Salt River Cemetery, Shepherd Isabella County MI. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Oak Grove Cemetery
notes: Oak Grove Cemetery, Hillsdale Hillsdale County MI. Welden family burial site. Distinct from CEM208 Oak Grove St. Louis MI. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Mount Hope Cemetery
notes: Mount Hope Cemetery, Litchfield Hillsdale County MI. Distinct from Mount Hope Cemeteries in Talcottville CT (CEM120), Rochester NY (CEM153), and Lansing MI (CEM210). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Resthaven Memorial Park
notes: Resthaven Memorial Park, Midland Midland County TX. First Texas burial in this Hooker line. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Lake Forest Cemetery
notes: Lake Forest Cemetery, Grand Haven Ottawa County MI. Burial place of Olger sisters Harriet E. Olger Walker and Cuma M. Olger. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Riverside Cemetery
notes: Riverside Cemetery, Albion Calhoun County MI. Distinct from Riverside Cemeteries in Farmington CT (CEM073), Asheville NC (CEM093), Rochester NY (CEM172), and Lanesborough MA (CEM199). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Spring Lake Cemetery
notes: Spring Lake Cemetery, Spring Lake Ottawa County MI. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Indian Fields Township Cemetery
notes: Indian Fields Township Cemetery, Caro Tuscola County MI. Burial place of Ward Joseph Walker (X02681). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Chapel Hill Memorial Gardens
notes: Chapel Hill Memorial Gardens, De Witt Clinton County MI. Burial place of Lois Louise Richmond Morey (HD3528) and her husband Harold Carl Morey. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Chapel Garden
notes: Chapel Garden, Elwell Gratiot County MI. Burial place of Lillian Mary Richmond Cottrill (HD3529). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: East Montcalm Cemetery
notes: East Montcalm Cemetery, Greenville Montcalm County MI. Burial place of Beatrice B. Olger Brown Coston (HD3533) and her second husband Leaton Murn Coston (X02694). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Eureka Township Cemetery
notes: Eureka Township Cemetery, Greenville Montcalm County MI. Burial place of Samuel Brown (X02693), Beatrice Olger's first husband. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Sheridan Cemetery
notes: Sheridan Cemetery, Sheridan Montcalm County MI. Burial place of Homer Raymond Olger (HD3535) and his wife Hazel J. Goodwin Olger (X02695). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Deepdale Memorial Park
notes: Deepdale Memorial Park, Delta Township Eaton County MI. Burial place of June Evangeline Welch Vandongen (HD3532) and her husband William E. Vandongen (X02692). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Saint Joseph Catholic Cemetery
notes: Saint Joseph Catholic Cemetery, Lansing Ingham County MI. Burial place of Derrell Ward Walker (HD3536) and his wife Doris Margaret Fessler Walker (X02696). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Forest Lawn Cemetery
notes: Forest Lawn Cemetery, Saginaw Saginaw County MI. Burial place of the Thompson twins (HD3537 Carroll O. Thompson and HD3538 Derrell O. Thompson) who both died in March 1921. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Lovejoy Cemetery
notes: Lovejoy Cemetery, Durand Shiawassee County MI. Burial place of Shirley Arlene Shriver Olger (X02697). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Oak Hill Cemetery
notes: Oak Hill Cemetery, Aloha Cheboygan County Michigan. Burial place of Eugene Oakleigh Fowler (HD3544) and his wife Joanna Jeanne May Fowler (X02702). Distinct from Oak Hill Cemeteries in Washington DC (CEM141, CEM169 — both pre-existing entries). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Oak Grove Memorial Cemetery
notes: Oak Grove Memorial Cemetery, Branford Suwannee County FL. Burial place of Marion Eugene Touchton (X02705), husband of Phyllis Elaine Parsons Touchton (HD3546). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Hopewell Cemetery
notes: Hopewell Cemetery, Moultrie Colquitt County GA. Burial place of Burnard Franklin Burgner (X02706), first husband of Jacqueline Onalee Parsons (HD3547) — buried at his Georgia family origin despite dying in Jacksonville FL. Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Fairview Cemetery
notes: Fairview Cemetery, Tipton Tipton County IN. Burial place of Evelyn May Myers Thomas (HD3548) and her husband Joseph C. Thomas (X02709). Distinct from Fairview Cemetery in New Britain CT (CEM011), Richfield OH (CEM055), West Hartford CT (CEM109), and Pampa TX (CEM179). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

name: Orlando Memorial Gardens
notes: Orlando Memorial Gardens, Apopka Orange County FL. Burial place of Janet Elaine Myers Hines (HD3549) and her husband Robert Floyd Hines (X02710). Per Descendants of Abel Fowler PDF.

Resting place of Cyrus Churchill (HD3555, 1826-1886) and his wife Louisa Harriett Wilson (X02726, 1837-1923) — the Connecticut-born wagon manufacturer who migrated to Rock Island in mid-19th century, and his Pennsylvania-born wife who arrived in Rock Island as a four-year-old.

Resting place of three consecutive generations of Herbert Churchills: Herbert Jenkins (HD3564, 1881-1934), his son Herbert Jenkins Jr. (HD3565, 1911-1977), and grandson Herbert J. III (HD3568, 1931-2006). Also Dora Mary Wood Churchill (X02730) and Edna Mae Ritchey Churchill Straub Weyant (X02731).

Major Gilbert family burial ground in Kensington CT — the village where Hooker Gilbert (H00420, 1751-1840) lived on the ancient Gilbert farm at 551 Christian Lane (built 1709 by Ebenezer Gilbert, sold to City of New Britain 1906). Resting place of Hooker Gilbert and both his wives, four of his Gilbert children with Candace Sage, and other extended Gilbert kin. Adjacent to the family's ancestral landmark.
Burial ground in Kensington CT — resting place of Franklin J. Elton (HD3573, 1858-1947) and his wife Hermina M. Adhemar (X02736, 1868-1938).
Burial ground in Norwich New London County CT — resting place of Bertram Earl Elton (HD3576, 1896-1969) and his wife Lila Newell Babcock (X02737, 1899-1999, age 100).

Resting place of the South Dover Hooker family across multiple generations: Dr. William Hooker (H01429, 1788-1853) and his cousin Amelia Stanley Hooker (HD0539, 1790-1885), their two children William Jr. (H02791) and Ann Hooker Vincent (H02792), William Jr.'s wife Sarah Varney Cutler Hooker (X01229) and four of their five children, plus Ann's husband Edwin Vincent (X02738) and her three Vincent children — Ella, Amelia H., Charles William — and onward through the Vincent line via Charles Kenneth and his wife Edith Akin Vincent down to Walter Akin Vincent (HD3593, WWII Navy). The 1814 cousin marriage united two Hooker branches via Amelia's TRIPLE Hooker descent.

Resting place of three generations of the Knickerbocker family in the Bangall/Stanfordville area of Dutchess County NY: Jennie Hooker Knickerbocker (H04402, 1860-1930) and her husband Edwin Knickerbocker (X01230, 1852-1930), their son Jay Knickerbocker (HD0544, 1890-1968) and his wife Florence Hillman Knickerbocker (X01231, 1893-1972), and their grandson Austin Jay Knickerbocker (HD0548, 1918-1997) along with his wife Helen Gertrude Germond Knickerbocker (X01232, 1918-2024 age 106). Austin's marker indicates his WWII veteran status.

Resting place of Helen Alice Clinton Vincent (X02741, 1923-2008), wife of Walter Akin Vincent (HD3593) — buried separately from her Vincent in-laws who rest at South Dover Rural Cemetery. Amenia is the neighboring town to Dover Plains where she and Walter spent their married life.

Primary Dykeman family burial ground in Pawling Dutchess County NY. Resting place of Peter Dykeman (X00958, 1858-1930, farmer and Pawling Baptist Church benefactor) and his wife Effie Keziah Hooker Dykeman (H04404, 1867-1936, triple Hooker descendant), and five of their seven children — William John, Peter Augustus, Calvin (infant), Alice C., and George Raymond — plus their youngest Henry Dykeman (HD0550, 1900-1984, roadside market co-founder and volunteer firefighter), his wife Velma Ellis (X00959), and Henry's son 'Farmer Hank' (HD3604, 1928-2017) with his wife Jean Wright.

Resting place of Marjorie Gladys Dykeman Cornell (HD0551, 1925-2010) and her husband Warren Lester Cornell (X01363, 1924-1994, WWII Army veteran). Both are triple Hooker descendants of Effie Keziah Hooker via the Dykeman line.

Resting place of the Danbury branch of the triple-Hooker-descent line. Ethel Mae Hooker McDermott (HD3605, 1898-1945) and Mabel Edna Hooker Germanetti (HD3606, 1902-1990) — daughters of John Calvin Hooker — are buried here with their husbands, and Mabel's daughter Jean Marie Germanetti Van Valkenburg (HD3609, 1930-2011) with her WWII Navy veteran husband Gilbert 'Bud' Van Valkenburg (X02747). Also Henry H. McDermott (HD3607, 1932-2013, Korean War Army veteran and Danbury postman) and George F. Schmiedel (X02746, 1930-2012, WWII veteran).

Family burial ground in Houseville, a hamlet of Turin Lewis County NY, named for the House family that migrated here from Glastonbury Connecticut in the early nineteenth century. Resting place of Betsey House Hills (HD0553, 1785-1866) and her husband Orange Hills (X01234, 1782-1848), her sister Clarissa House Bush (HD0554, 1786-1813, dead at twenty-six) and her husband Henry Bush (X01365, 1781-1837), and Betsey's son Edwin Hills (HD0556, 1830-1892).

Town cemetery in Turin Lewis County NY — distinct from the smaller Houseville Cemetery (CEM247). Resting place of Cyprian House (HD3619, 1797-1883), youngest child of Esther Hooker House, and his wife Sophia Risley House (X02754) along with their three Turin-buried children Lovinia, Henry Risley, and Chester. Also Roxanna 'Roxy' Hills House (HD0555) and her first-cousin husband Morgan House (HD3620, 1803-1899, age 96), and Chester's son Eugene Madison House (HD3643, 1861-1950, photo) with his wife Ida Isabel Benjamin (X02759, died at 39).

Resting place of Henry Algernon Hills (HD3625, 1835-1908) — Civil War quartermaster, twenty-five-year teacher, and Calamus Wisconsin pioneer settler — and his wife Mary A. Wheelock Hills (X02752, 1841-1920) who raised eight children with him in a Dodge County log house starting in 1857.
Resting place of Rev. Henry Wheelock Hills (HD3631, 1869-1949) and his wife Lucy Lucille Stoll Hills (X02753, 1878-1952) — Wisconsin-born clergyman and his wife.

Resting place of the Doud branch of the House family in Monroe County NY, including Emma Jane House Johnson (HD3642, formerly of Glenfield) who married Emmett Johnson, Leon S. Doud (HD3647, WWII Normandy veteran who died at thirty-four), his sister Josephine Doud Gursslin (HD3648, 1922-2013) and her husband Alvin Dewitt Gursslin (X02762), and Leon S.'s son Rodney Lee Doud (HD3650, 1947-1977).

Resting place of Olive Edna House Doud (HD3646, 1900-1970) — great-great-granddaughter of Esther Hooker House — and her husband Leon William Doud (X02760, 1885-1962), in Martinsburg Lewis County NY where Leon was born.
Resting place of Louise Doud Duchins (HD3649, 1929-2022, age 92-93), daughter of Olive Edna House Doud, in Rochester Monroe County NY.

Historic burying ground in Farmington Connecticut, founded 1661 — one of the oldest cemeteries in the state. Resting place of John Mix (X00263, 1755-1834, Revolutionary War lieutenant and 32-year Farmington town clerk) and his wife Martha Cowles Mix (H00437, 1753-1826), with most of their eight children — Capt. Ebenezer Hooker Mix (the East India sailor character of Farmington), Betsey, Catherine Williams, John William, and Martha Hooker — alongside Ann Goodwin (the first, lost at age 3) and the boy William (lost at age 4). Ann Goodwin No. 2 also rests here.

Riverside Cemetery in Farmington Hartford County CT. Resting place of multiple Deming-line descendants of Catherine Hooker Williams Deming (H02325): her husband John Deming (I00363, 1825-1894), their son Edward Hooker Deming (H02329, 1857-1928) and his wife Isabella Plum Deming (I00365, 1856-1933), and their son Samuel Lewis Deming (H02328, 1855-1871, dead at sixteen).

Spring Grove Cemetery in Darien Fairfield County CT. Resting place of Harry Wadsworth Deming (H02332, 1868-1934) and his wife Helen L. Hoyt Deming (I00366, 1873-1932). Harry's middle name 'Wadsworth' was likely chosen for his great-uncle-by-marriage Dr. Harry Wadsworth (X00267, 1780-1813), husband of his great-grandmother's sister Ann Goodwin Mix.

Warwick Cemetery in Warwick Orange County NY. Resting place of Annie Williams Deming Ennis (H02327, 1851-1938) and her husband Thomas Ridgway Ennis (I00364, 1853-1930) — Annie was the eldest surviving daughter of John Deming (I00363) and Catherine Hooker Williams Deming (H02325).

Cholera Cemetery (originally Harrison Street Cemetery) in Sandusky Erie County OH. Sherman Pierpont (H00825, 1783-1836) was buried here after his body washed ashore on Lake Erie May 27, 1836 — he had drowned May 7, 1836 when a sailboat capsized in a squall off Put-in-Bay (South Bass Island, then called Edwards Island). The first Sandusky cemetery had been closed by 1836; the new Harrison Street Cemetery was the only public burial ground in use at the time of his death.

Northfield Cemetery in Northfield Litchfield County CT. Resting place of Sidney Humiston Pierpont (I00369, 1786-1841), Sherman Pierpont's widow, and her daughter Minerva Pierpont Woodward (HD1179, 1809-1837 died at 27 leaving two young daughters).

Evergreen Cemetery in Watertown Litchfield County CT. Resting place of Sherman Prindle Woodward (I00370, 1807-1896, age 89, Litchfield civic leader and 25-year senior warden of Christ Church) and his second wife Hannah Eliza Russell Woodward (X02772, 1808-1899, age 91 — extraordinary longevity).

East Side Burying Ground in Woodbridge New Haven County CT. Resting place of Rachael Prindle Woodward Barker (HD3653, 1833-1917), her husband Dr. John William Barker (I00374, 1835-1907), and their two daughters Rachael P. Barker (HD3655, 1875-1981 — age 106) and Grace Sylvia Barker (HD3656, 1878-1978 — age 99 — both extraordinary longevity, sisters spanning a century together).

West Cemetery in Plymouth Litchfield County CT. Resting place of George Pierpont (HD1180, 1819-1884), youngest child of Sherman Pierpont — who continued the Pierpont line in Connecticut after his father's drowning death in Ohio.

John Langdon (X00389, 1790-1830) — Yale 1809 Salutatory Orator, pastor of Bethlehem Congregational Church 1816-1825 who admitted 83 members during his tenure. He married two Pierpont sisters in succession: Elizabeth (H00828) and after her death her younger sister Abby (H00830).

Evergreen Cemetery in New Haven New Haven County CT. NOT to be confused with Evergreen Cemetery in Watertown CT (CEM260). Resting place of Dr. Timothy Langdon (HD1248, 1818-1874, Yale Medical School 1841, Naugatuck physician 1841-1867 then New Haven 1867-1874) and his eldest surviving daughter Caroline Elizabeth Langdon Meyers (HD3658, 1844-1888).

name: Sacramento Historic City Cemetery

name: Cypress Lawn Memorial Park

Resting place of the Bridewell family branch — Mary Frances Badger Bridewell (HD3754, Hooker descendant gen-10), her husband David Alexander Bridewell (I00421, Chicago attorney), their adopted son Jonathan Lee Bridewell (X02777), and biological son Alexander Hunt Bridewell (HD3756, gen-11). name: Oakland Cemetery

Werner Sr (I00425), and all four of their children — Edward Jr (HD3775), Lynn Woodruff (HD3776), Florence Brine (HD3777), Ray Cowles (HD3778) — plus Ray's wife Gertrude Comly Werner (I00427). name: Decatur Cemetery
notes: Historic cemetery in Decatur Georgia. Burial place of Col. Milton Anthony Candler (X02780, US Congressman 1875-79) and his wife Eliza Murphey Candler (X02781, daughter of US Representative Charles Murphey). Also burial place of many DeKalb County notables; Decatur was the original DeKalb county seat.

name: Westview Cemetery

Burial place of Florence Candler Cowles (I00429, 1867-1940, daughter of Congressman Milton Candler), her daughter Florence Cowles Hardisty (HD3782, d.1920 age 27), son-in-law John T. Hardisty Sr (I00431, d.1938), and grandson John Thomas Hardisty Jr (HD3784, d.1927 age 9). Also contains Henry Grady, Joel Chandler Harris, Asa Griggs Candler (Coca-Cola founder + brother-in-law of Milton Candler).

name: Restland Memorial Park

name: Oak Hill Cemetery

Henry Chandler was originally interred at Oak Woods Cemetery, Chicago in September 1939; his remains were transferred to Cave Hill in 1982 to rest beside Elizabeth (per Victor M. Cassidy, 'Henry Chandler Cowles, Pioneer Ecologist,' p. 346). The transfer reunited the family in Elizabeth's hometown.

name: Evergreen Cemetery

name: Derby Center Cemetery

name: Cimetière Mont-Royal

Burial site of multiple members of the Lyman family — Hannah Stiles Lyman's descendants who became prominent Montreal druggists, lawyers, and civic figures: Elisha Lyman Jr. (with his three wives memorialized together), Henry Lyman, Mary Corse Lyman, Henry Herbert Lyman (later commemorated here after death on the Empress of Ireland 1914), Albert Clarence Lyman, Frederick Stiles Lyman, Edith Mary Louise Lyman, and others.

name: Saint Johns In The Wilderness Episcopal Cemetery

name: Ross Bay Cemetery

name: Capilano View Cemetery
